pattern: I remember when ...
b1: I remember when I first met my best friend.
I remember when we went to the beach last summer.
I remember when I bought my very first bike.
I remember when it snowed a lot in our town.
I remember when I was a little child in elementary school.
b2: I remember when I had to give a speech in front of a large audience.
I remember when our family decided to move to a different city.
I remember when I finally passed my driving test after two tries.
I remember when technology wasn't as advanced as it is today.
I remember when I got my first job and felt so proud.
c1: I remember when the political landscape of the country shifted overnight.
I remember when I first realized the profound impact of social inequality.
I remember when a single conversation completely altered my career path.
I remember when the city underwent a massive urban transformation.
I remember when I struggled to balance my personal values with professional demands.

pattern: Once upon a time, ...
b1: Once upon a time, there lived a king in a big castle.
Once upon a time, I wanted to be an astronaut.
Once upon a time, this park was a small farm.
Once upon a time, we didn't have any smartphones.
Once upon a time, a little girl found a magic ring.
b2: Once upon a time, this neighborhood was a quiet place with many trees.
Once upon a time, I believed that anything was possible with enough hard work.
Once upon a time, people used to write long letters to each other.
Once upon a time, there was a small startup that changed the world.
Once upon a time, travel was much slower and more difficult than now.
c1: Once upon a time, the prevailing wisdom was that the economy would grow indefinitely.
Once upon a time, this region was the epicenter of a flourishing artistic movement.
Once upon a time, society held much more rigid views on gender roles.
Once upon a time, the concept of privacy was viewed through a completely different lens.
Once upon a time, the boundaries between work and personal life were clearly defined.

pattern: The story begins with ...
b1: The story begins with a young boy in a small village.
The story begins with a rainy day in London.
The story begins with a mysterious letter in the mail.
The story begins with my family going on a vacation.
The story begins with a cat lost in the forest.
b2: The story begins with the main character losing his job unexpectedly.
The story begins with a chance meeting at a crowded airport.
The story begins with a decision that changed everything for the hero.
The story begins with a childhood memory that haunts the narrator.
The story begins with a discovery of an old diary in the attic.
c1: The story begins with a detailed description of the protagonist's existential crisis.
The story begins with the subtle breakdown of a long-standing diplomatic alliance.
The story begins with a philosophical inquiry into the nature of truth.
The story begins with an intricate portrayal of 19th-century societal norms.
The story begins with a series of seemingly unrelated events that later converge.

pattern: That reminds me of a time when ...
b1: That reminds me of a time when I got lost in the mall.
That reminds me of a time when we cooked dinner together.
That reminds me of a time when I visited Paris.
That reminds me of a time when I won a gold medal.
That reminds me of a time when my dog ran away.
b2: That reminds me of a time when I had to survive a week without electricity.
That reminds me of a time when I completely misunderstood a cultural gesture.
That reminds me of a time when I worked on a very challenging project.
That reminds me of a time when our car broke down in the middle of nowhere.
That reminds me of a time when I had a serious disagreement with my boss.
c1: That reminds me of a time when the entire organization faced an ethical dilemma.
That reminds me of a time when I witnessed a remarkable act of collective resilience.
That reminds me of a time when I had to navigate a complex bureaucratic system.
That reminds me of a time when intellectual curiosity led to an unexpected discovery.
That reminds me of a time when the limitations of human communication became evident.

pattern: Let me tell you about ...
b1: Let me tell you about my favorite hobby.
Let me tell you about my trip to Jeju island.
Let me tell you about my wonderful grandmother.
Let me tell you about my first pet dog.
Let me tell you about a funny thing that happened today.
b2: Let me tell you about the most difficult challenge I've ever faced.
Let me tell you about how I learned to speak three languages.
Let me tell you about a person who inspired me to change my life.
Let me tell you about the time I volunteered in a foreign country.
Let me tell you about a project that didn't go as planned.
c1: Let me tell you about the intricate complexities of managing a global team.
Let me tell you about a transformative experience that reshaped my worldview.
Let me tell you about the nuances involved in this scientific breakthrough.
Let me tell you about a historical event that continues to influence our society.
Let me tell you about the profound psychological shifts that occur during a crisis.

pattern: We were just about to ... when suddenly ...
b1: We were just about to eat when suddenly the phone rang.
We were just about to leave when suddenly it started to rain.
We were just about to sleep when suddenly we heard a loud noise.
We were just about to start the game when suddenly the power went out.
We were just about to go out when suddenly my mom arrived.
b2: We were just about to board the plane when suddenly we realized we lost a passport.
We were just about to sign the contract when suddenly a new problem appeared.
We were just about to finish the hike when suddenly the weather turned dangerous.
We were just about to announce the winner when suddenly there was an objection.
We were just about to launch the app when suddenly a major bug was found.
c1: We were just about to finalize the merger when suddenly the market crashed.
We were just about to reach a consensus when suddenly a new piece of evidence emerged.
We were just about to publish the findings when suddenly we discovered a flaw in the data.
We were just about to initiate the protocol when suddenly an alarm went off.
We were just about to conclude the debate when suddenly a provocative question was raised.

pattern: And then, the most unexpected thing happened: ...
translation: 그러고 나서 가장 예상치 못한 일이 일어났어요. ...
b1: And then, the most unexpected thing happened: I found my lost keys.
And then, the most unexpected thing happened: it started to snow in summer.
And then, the most unexpected thing happened: he gave me a present.
And then, the most unexpected thing happened: I won the lottery.
And then, the most unexpected thing happened: the bird spoke to me.
b2: And then, the most unexpected thing happened: an old friend I hadn't seen in years walked in.
And then, the most unexpected thing happened: the engine started working again by itself.
And then, the most unexpected thing happened: I was offered a promotion on the spot.
And then, the most unexpected thing happened: the crowd fell completely silent.
And then, the most unexpected thing happened: the stray dog led us to the main road.
c1: And then, the most unexpected thing happened: the primary suspect confessed to the crime.
And then, the most unexpected thing happened: a breakthrough occurred in the middle of the night.
And then, the most unexpected thing happened: the long-stalled negotiations were suddenly resolved.
And then, the most unexpected thing happened: the experimental results contradicted all previous theories.
And then, the most unexpected thing happened: a seemingly insignificant detail proved to be the key.

pattern: I'll never forget the moment when ...
translation: ...했던 그 순간을 절대 잊지 못할 거예요.
b1: I'll never forget the moment when I saw the ocean for the first time.
I'll never forget the moment when my little brother was born.
I'll never forget the moment when I finished my first marathon.
I'll never forget the moment when we moved into our new house.
I'll never forget the moment when I passed my final exam.
b2: I'll never forget the moment when I realized I was finally independent.
I'll never forget the moment when the audience gave me a standing ovation.
I'll never forget the moment when I reached the summit of the mountain.
I'll never forget the moment when I held my newborn child for the first time.
I'll never forget the moment when I stood on the stage at graduation.
c1: I'll never forget the moment when I witnessed the sheer scale of the humanitarian effort.
I'll never forget the moment when the true gravity of the situation became apparent.
I'll never forget the moment when I finally grasped the complexity of the philosophical concept.
I'll never forget the moment when the visionary leader delivered his final address.
I'll never forget the moment when I decided to leave my comfortable life for a new challenge.

pattern: At first, I thought ..., but later I realized ...
b1: At first, I thought he was mean, but later I realized he was just shy.
At first, I thought the exam was easy, but later I realized it was hard.
At first, I thought I lost my phone, but later I realized it was in my bag.
At first, I thought it was a dog, but later I realized it was a cat.
At first, I thought I was late, but later I realized I was early.
b2: At first, I thought moving abroad would be easy, but later I realized how much I'd miss home.
At first, I thought the project was a failure, but later I realized we learned a lot.
At first, I thought the book was boring, but later I realized it was a masterpiece.
At first, I thought she was joking, but later I realized she was being serious.
At first, I thought money was everything, but later I realized that health is more important.
c1: At first, I thought the data was conclusive, but later I realized there were significant biases.
At first, I thought the policy would be effective, but later I realized it had many loopholes.
At first, I thought the two events were related, but later I realized it was just a coincidence.
At first, I thought leadership was about authority, but later I realized it's about empowerment.
At first, I thought the technology was a panacea, but later I realized it brought new ethical challenges.

pattern: Everything changed after ...
b1: Everything changed after I met my teacher.
Everything changed after we moved to the city.
Everything changed after I got a new computer.
Everything changed after the big storm.
Everything changed after I started to exercise.
b2: Everything changed after the company announced its new strategy.
Everything changed after I decided to quit my stable job.
Everything changed after the invention of the smartphone.
Everything changed after I spent a year traveling alone.
Everything changed after we lost our most important client.
c1: Everything changed after the geopolitical shifts in the region.
Everything changed after the implementation of the new economic reforms.
Everything changed after a major breakthrough in renewable energy technology.
Everything changed after the public discourse moved towards environmental awareness.
Everything changed after I experienced a profound personal loss that reshaped my priorities.

pattern: Before I knew it, ...
b1: Before I knew it, the sun was going down.
Before I knew it, I was finished with my homework.
Before I knew it, summer vacation was over.
Before I knew it, the bus had arrived.
Before I knew it, I had eaten the whole pizza.
b2: Before I knew it, I had become an expert in coding.
Before I knew it, the project was due and I was rushing to finish.
Before I knew it, three years had passed since I moved here.
Before I knew it, the situation had escalated into a major argument.
Before I knew it, I was fluent enough to have a conversation in Spanish.
c1: Before I knew it, the small startup had evolved into a multi-national corporation.
Before I knew it, the initial enthusiasm had been replaced by a sense of disillusionment.
Before I knew it, the subtle changes in the climate had become impossible to ignore.
Before I knew it, I had been drawn into a complex web of political intrigue.
Before I knew it, the conversation had shifted from casual talk to a deep philosophical debate.

pattern: The whole experience taught me ...
translation: 그 모든 경험은 제게 ...라는 것을 가르쳐 주었어요.
b1: The whole experience taught me to be patient.
The whole experience taught me that family is important.
The whole experience taught me how to cook.
The whole experience taught me to work hard.
The whole experience taught me that I love hiking.
b2: The whole experience taught me the importance of effective communication.
The whole experience taught me how to handle failure with grace.
The whole experience taught me that I am much stronger than I thought.
The whole experience taught me to appreciate the small things in life.
The whole experience taught me that preparation is the key to success.
c1: The whole experience taught me the fundamental value of intellectual humility.
The whole experience taught me how systemic biases can affect decision-making.
The whole experience taught me the intricate balance between ambition and well-being.
The whole experience taught me that resilience is built through adversity and reflection.
The whole experience taught me to scrutinize my own assumptions before judging others.

pattern: The first thing that happened was ...
b1: The first thing that happened was the lights went off.
The first thing that happened was he said hello to me.
The first thing that happened was I woke up late.
The first thing that happened was we lost our map.
The first thing that happened was it started to rain.
b2: The first thing that happened was that the flight was delayed by four hours.
The first thing that happened was a misunderstanding about the meeting time.
The first thing that happened was the manager introduced the new team members.
The first thing that happened was we noticed a strange sound coming from the engine.
The first thing that happened was a sudden drop in the stock prices.
c1: The first thing that happened was a complete breakdown in communication between the parties.
The first thing that happened was the emergence of an unexpected technical glitch.
The first thing that happened was a thorough review of the existing protocols.
The first thing that happened was the implementation of emergency safety measures.
The first thing that happened was a profound shift in public opinion regarding the issue.

pattern: Looking back now, I can see that ...
translation: 지금 되돌아보니 ...라는 것을 알 수 있어요.
b1: Looking back now, I can see that I was very young.
Looking back now, I can see that she was a good friend.
Looking back now, I can see that the trip was great.
Looking back now, I can see that I made a mistake.
Looking back now, I can see that hard work paid off.
b2: Looking back now, I can see that the challenge made me a better person.
Looking back now, I can see that we should have listened to her advice.
Looking back now, I can see that the decision was the right one at the time.
Looking back now, I can see that I was focusing on the wrong things.
Looking back now, I can see that every failure was a stepping stone to success.
c1: Looking back now, I can see that the socio-economic factors played a crucial role.
Looking back now, I can see that the initial hypothesis was based on flawed logic.
Looking back now, I can see that the experience was a pivotal moment in my career.
Looking back now, I can see that the signs of the crisis were already there.
Looking back now, I can see that my perspective was limited by my own cultural background.

pattern: I felt completely ...
b1: I felt completely happy when I won.
I felt completely tired after the walk.
I felt completely lost in the city.
I felt completely surprised by the gift.
I felt completely fine after the doctor's visit.
b2: I felt completely overwhelmed by the amount of work I had to do.
I felt completely relieved when the project was finally over.
I felt completely confident after practicing my presentation.
I felt completely exhausted after the long-distance flight.
I felt completely satisfied with the results of the experiment.
c1: I felt completely disillusioned after witnessing the corruption within the system.
I felt completely invigorated by the intellectual exchange at the conference.
I felt completely vindicated when the data finally supported my controversial theory.
I felt completely immersed in the local culture during my stay.
I felt completely alienated from the corporate environment and its values.

pattern: It was the moment when ...
b1: It was the moment when I found my lost ring.
It was the moment when the sun came out.
It was the moment when I scored a goal.
It was the moment when she said 'Yes'.
It was the moment when the music stopped.
b2: It was the moment when I realized that I wanted to be a doctor.
It was the moment when all our hard work finally started to pay off.
It was the moment when the truth finally came to light.
It was the moment when I decided to take a big risk for my career.
It was the moment when the two countries signed the peace treaty.
c1: It was the moment when the culmination of years of research bore fruit.
It was the moment when the inherent contradictions of the plan became undeniable.
It was the moment when a single act of courage inspired an entire movement.
It was the moment when the delicate balance of power finally shifted.
It was the moment when I fully understood the ethical weight of my decision.

pattern: To cut a long story short, ...
b1: To cut a long story short, we got home safely.
To cut a long story short, I bought the car.
To cut a long story short, the party was great.
To cut a long story short, we missed the train.
To cut a long story short, I passed the test.
b2: To cut a long story short, we decided to cancel the project due to the budget.
To cut a long story short, the two companies reached an agreement.
To cut a long story short, I ended up moving to London for a year.
To cut a long story short, the situation was resolved after many meetings.
To cut a long story short, the vacation wasn't as relaxing as we hoped.
c1: To cut a long story short, the experiment failed to produce the expected results.
To cut a long story short, the legal battle lasted for years without a clear winner.
To cut a long story short, the organizational restructuring led to significant layoffs.
To cut a long story short, the theoretical framework proved to be inadequate for the task.
To cut a long story short, the merger was called off due to cultural incompatibilities.

pattern: The atmosphere was very ...
b1: The atmosphere was very happy at the party.
The atmosphere was very quiet in the library.
The atmosphere was very cold in the room.
The atmosphere was very friendly at the cafe.
The atmosphere was very tense during the game.
b2: The atmosphere was very welcoming and warm in the small village.
The atmosphere was very competitive during the final match.
The atmosphere was very professional and organized at the conference.
The atmosphere was very relaxed during our summer holiday.
The atmosphere was very energetic at the rock concert.
c1: The atmosphere was very solemn during the memorial service.
The atmosphere was very sophisticated and intellectually stimulating.
The atmosphere was very electric with anticipation before the announcement.
The atmosphere was very stagnant and lacked creative inspiration.
The atmosphere was very inclusive, fostering a sense of belonging for everyone.

pattern: If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say ...
b1: If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'fun'.
If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'scary'.
If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'beautiful'.
If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'easy'.
If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'fast'.
b2: If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'exhausting'.
If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'inspiring'.
If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'unpredictable'.
If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'challenging'.
If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'rewarding'.
c1: If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'transformative'.
If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'paradoxical'.
If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'sublime'.
If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'inconclusive'.
If I had to describe it in one word, I'd say 'ephemeral'.

pattern: The funniest part of the whole thing was ...
b1: The funniest part of the whole thing was when my dad fell asleep.
The funniest part of the whole thing was the talking bird.
The funniest part of the whole thing was my dog wearing a hat.
The funniest part of the whole thing was when he forgot his name.
The funniest part of the whole thing was the clown's dance.
b2: The funniest part of the whole thing was our reaction when we saw the bill.
The funniest part of the whole thing was the look on his face when he was surprised.
The funniest part of the whole thing was trying to cook with no instructions.
The funniest part of the whole thing was the unexpected guest at the wedding.
The funniest part of the whole thing was my attempt to speak the local dialect.
c1: The funniest part of the whole thing was the sheer irony of the situation.
The funniest part of the whole thing was the way the tension was broken by a silly remark.
The funniest part of the whole thing was the series of absurd coincidences that led to the event.
The funniest part of the whole thing was the self-deprecating humor of the speaker.
The funniest part of the whole thing was the contrast between the formal setting and the chaotic events.
